summary: Archer Aviation optimal portfolio weight is 0.0–1.0% (recommended maximum 1.0% for tactical exposure). Negative expected return of -44.3% makes ACHR unsuitable for inclusion in Sharpe-maximizing portfolios. At 1% weight, portfolio Sharpe declines from 0.233 to 0.170 (-27.2%), with -51.3 bp drag on return and +261 bp increase in volatility. Allocation justified only for investors with explicit thematic mandates and conviction on bull case execution (15% probability).

extras.rationale: ACHR has negative expected return (-44.3%) and high volatility (60%). Sharpe-maximizing framework would recommend 0% weight. However, 0.5–1.0% tactical allocation may be justified for investors with aerospace/eVTOL thematic mandates and conviction on bull case forward driver execution (Abu Dhabi Q3 2026 launch, FAA Type Cert Q4 2026, battery 400 Wh/kg confirmation; combined ~15% probability per A5.1).
extras.methodology: Markowitz 2-asset optimization with S&P 500 baseline (7% return, 15% vol, 0.50 correlation with ACHR). At 0% weight: Sharpe = 0.2333 (baseline). At 1% weight: Sharpe = 0.1698 (27% decline). At 2% weight: Sharpe = 0.1247 (46% decline). Risk-parity allocation (20% ACHR) yields negative return (-3.26%) and is rejected.
conditions_to_increase_above_1pct: Abu Dhabi operations confirmed operational and scaling per Q3 2026 launch (A3_3.md milestone)
FAA Type Certification delivered on schedule (late 2026, per A3_3.md)
Production ramp validated at 150+ aircraft/year (approaching base case execution per A5_1.md)
Capital raise confirmed non-dilutive (Stellantis partnership terms locked per A2_3.md)

extras.interpretation: At 1% weight, ACHR reduces portfolio return by 51.3 bp. The negative contribution reflects the asset's negative expected return, which is driven by 60% base/bear case probability (A5.1: 60% × -49.7% + 25% × -81.8%) dominating the 15% bull case probability (+33.1%).
extras.source_expected_return: A5.1 three-scenario valuation: Bull 15% × (+33.1%) + Base 60% × (-49.7%) + Bear 25% × (-81.8%) = -44.3%

rows.label: Marginal Contribution to Risk
rows.value: 21.0 bp marginal risk + 261 bp total portfolio volatility increase (at 1.0% weight)

extras.risk_amplification: Portfolio volatility increases 261 bp (1.7x the marginal contribution of 21 bp) due to ACHR's standalone volatility (60%) being 4x the baseline (15%) and moderate correlation (0.5). This non-linear amplification makes ACHR an inefficient risk contributor.
extras.sharpe_deterioration_from_risk: Higher portfolio volatility (+261 bp) combined with lower return (-51 bp) reduces Sharpe ratio from 0.2333 to 0.1698 (27% decline).

rows.label: Rationale
rows.value: Archer's negative 12-month expected return (-44.3%) and high volatility (60%) make it unsuitable for return-optimized portfolios. Even 1% allocation reduces portfolio Sharpe by 27% (from 0.233 to 0.170), equivalent to a 51 bp return drag with 261 bp volatility increase. Allocation above 1% is not justified by current risk-return profile. Minimal 0.5–1.0% weight may be justified only for investors with explicit thematic mandates (aerospace/eVTOL) and conviction on bull case execution (15% probability).

key_findings: ACHR has 60% probability of negative returns (base case -49.7%, bear case -81.8% per A5.1)

Bull case (+33.1%) has only 15% probability and requires simultaneous execution on three forward drivers (Abu Dhabi launch, FAA Type Cert, battery confirmation per A3.3 combined ~39% all-hit probability)
ACHR is highly sensitive to labor market shocks: base case +1σ wage inflation shock drives stock from $3.25 to $1.13 (65% loss per A5.2)
Risk-parity allocation (20% ACHR) yields portfolio return below risk-free rate (-3.26%), resulting in deeply negative Sharpe ratio (-0.39)

recommendation_framework.sharpe_optimizing_portfolio: 0% weight (negative expected return disqualifies from inclusion)
recommendation_framework.thematic_mandate_portfolio: 0.5–1.0% tactical weight with quarterly milestone review
recommendation_framework.conviction_bull_case_investor: Up to 1.0% as satellite position; increase above 1% only upon milestone confirmation (Abu Dhabi operational, Type Cert delivered, production 150+ aircraft)

increase_above_1pct_if: Abu Dhabi Q3 2026 commercial launch confirmed operational (flights begin, revenue recognized)
FAA Type Certification delivered (late 2026 per A3.3)
Production ramp validated at 150+ aircraft annualized rate (base case achievement per A5.1)
Capital raise confirmed non-dilutive terms (Stellantis partnership locked per A2.3)
decrease_to_zero_if: Abu Dhabi launch slips beyond Q4 2026 (triggers base/bear case transition per A3.3)
FAA Type Cert slips >6 months (Q2 2027 or later; bear case scenario per A3.3)
Production ramp misses 100 aircraft/year run rate (cascades to capital crisis per A5.2)
Additional $500M+ dilutive capital raise forced at distressed valuation (per A5.1 bear case)

benchmark_comparison.typical_growth_etf_weight: 0% to 0.5% (eVTOL sector exposure via Joby/Beta/Lilium, not Archer)
benchmark_comparison.arkk_estimated_archer_weight: <0.5% (Joby preferred over Archer due to regulatory advantage per A4.2)
benchmark_comparison.msci_world_growth_weight: 0% (Archer not in index; micro-cap not captured)
benchmark_comparison.recommendation: Do not use as core holding. Cap at 1% as speculative satellite position with quarterly review tied to forward driver milestones.
